task_completion.description: Import the transaction-service project into Eclipse.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Eclipse IDE is open with the 'transaction-service' project visible in the Project Explorer or Package Explorer pane.

task_completion.description: Configure the JaCoCo Maven plugin in pom.xml with a 70% minimum line coverage rule.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The pom.xml file is open in an editor showing the jacoco-maven-plugin configuration, specifically including a <rule> with a <minimum>0.70</minimum> (or 70%) threshold for line coverage.

task_completion.description: Write JUnit 5 tests using Mockito for TransactionService, FeeCalculator, and TransactionValidator.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Java test files (e.g., TransactionServiceTest.java) are visible in the editor containing JUnit 5 annotations (like @Test) and Mockito annotations/methods (like @Mock for TransactionRepository/AccountRepository, @InjectMocks, or Mockito.when).

task_completion.description: Run the test phase to generate the JaCoCo HTML report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal or Eclipse console shows a successful Maven build execution (e.g., 'mvn test' or 'mvn clean test') with logs indicating that tests were run and the jacoco:report goal was executed.

task_completion.description: Ensure line coverage reaches at least 70% in the generated JaCoCo HTML report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser or Eclipse internal browser is open displaying the JaCoCo HTML report (index.html), showing the coverage table for the project classes with the Total line coverage percentage at 70% or higher.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used Eclipse IDE as requested rather than bypassing it.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the Eclipse IDE interface being used to view or edit the project files, not just a terminal or a different text editor like VS Code.

integrity.description: Verify the coverage report was genuinely generated by Maven and JaCoCo.
integrity.visual_evidence: Console or terminal logs should show actual Maven test execution and JaCoCo plugin output. The HTML report should look like a standard JaCoCo generated page, not a manually created or edited HTML file.

integrity.description: Verify the test code contains actual testing logic.
integrity.visual_evidence: The visible test code should show meaningful assertions and mock setups for deposits, withdrawals, or fee calculations, rather than empty test methods designed just to pass a file existence check.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project is located at /home/ga/transaction-service. The test framework must be JUnit 5 and Mockito. The minimum coverage threshold is 70%. The JaCoCo HTML report is expected at target/site/jacoco/index.html. Key classes to be tested are TransactionService, FeeCalculator, and TransactionValidator. The initial project has 0 test files.
